location.official_address: National Institute of Technology Calicut, NIT Campus P.O., Kozhikode, Kerala 673601
location.nearest_bus_terminal: KSRTC Central Bus Station / Corporation Bus Station, Palayam
location.nearest_major_hospital: KMCT Medical College Hospital, Manassery (external); NITC Health Centre is on campus
location.technology_ecosystem: Residential institute campus with Technology Business Incubator and multiple research/innovation centres; Kozhikode city is about 22 km away
location.data_origin: Official JoSAA/NITC distances and transport text; coordinates are map-derived and require manual confirmation

senior_junior_culture.summary: No strong, independently repeated evidence was found to support a definitive claim. One comparison thread reports that ragging was not a problem, while official sources show elected student bodies and formal student-support structures. Treat senior-junior culture as a limited anecdotal signal rather than a verified campus-wide fact.
positive_themes: peer guidance is available in some student discussions
formal SAC, guidance and anti-ragging structures exist officially
negative_themes: experiences may differ by hostel, batch and branch

academic_culture.summary: Official sources describe an autonomous credit-based curriculum with laboratories, computing and library infrastructure. Student discussions generally characterize academics and infrastructure as adequate to strong, but the source sample is too small for claims about strictness, grading pressure or faculty quality by department.
negative_themes: workload/strictness varies and was not established
academic_culture.evidence_strength: Moderate official evidence; limited student-reported evidence

hostel_reality.summary: The institute officially reports 15 hostels with dining, common/reading rooms and internet. Visual tours and discussions indicate meaningful variation between old and newer blocks; some rooms are described as basic or congested. Mess experience is subjective and may vary by hostel.
positive_themes: multiple hostels and attached dining halls
visual evidence of functioning hostel spaces
negative_themes: room size and maintenance vary by block
some older rooms described as average/congested
mess quality is inconsistent across personal reports
hostel_reality.evidence_strength: Strong official facility evidence; moderate repeated qualitative signal for block-to-block variation

club_and_campus_life.summary: Official sources list a broad range of technical, cultural, service and sports clubs, and the institute runs Tathva and Ragam. Visual sources consistently show a large, green residential campus. Activity levels should still be rechecked club by club.
negative_themes: some club pages lack recent dated activity
club_and_campus_life.evidence_strength: Strong official signal; moderate visual/student-reported support

coding_and_placement_culture.summary: The official presence of CPHUB, FOSSCell, AI Club, RIG and CCD training supports an active technical-preparation ecosystem. Student reports are mixed on how strong coding culture feels outside computing-focused peer groups. Official NIRF outcomes show solid overall placements, but branch-specific placement rates and eligibility are not available in the verified dataset.
positive_themes: coding programs and hackathons from CCD
negative_themes: student perception of coding culture varies by branch and peer group
coding_and_placement_culture.evidence_strength: Strong official infrastructure/outcome signal; conflicting student-reported culture signal

locality_and_student_life.summary: The campus is scenic and residential but approximately 22 km from Kozhikode city. Official sources report frequent local buses and road access; qualitative reports suggest late-night city travel may be less convenient than daytime travel.
positive_themes: on-campus banks, post office, health care and shops
negative_themes: late-night public transport may be limited
locality_and_student_life.evidence_strength: Strong official distance/connectivity evidence; limited anecdotal late-night signal

overall_satisfaction.summary: Available evidence points to a generally positive academic and residential-campus proposition, with the strongest recurring cautions being hostel-block variation, distance from the city and uneven branch-specific perceptions of coding/placement culture. The qualitative source base is not large enough to assign a numeric satisfaction rating.

verified_strengths: Central Government-funded Institute of National Importance
2026 official seat matrix and fee circular available
overall NIRF placement medians increased from 10.8 to 12.5 LPA across the three reported graduating cohorts
residential campus with health care, banks, post office and hostels
parent_mode_summary.cost_view: For B.Tech, calculated four-year institute fee is approximately ₹1.63 lakh, ₹3.30 lakh or ₹6.63 lakh depending on tuition-waiver category, excluding hostel/mess. Fixed hostel charges are ₹50,000 per year plus one-time charges and mess advances.
parent_mode_summary.label: Official verified plus clearly labelled calculated values

student_mode_summary.academic_and_career: Strong official academic infrastructure, competitive-programming/technical clubs and a broad recruiter list. Outcomes are best interpreted overall, not as a promise for a specific branch.
student_mode_summary.campus_life: Large green residential campus with technical/cultural festivals, clubs and sports; city access takes time.
student_mode_summary.hostel_reality: Hostel experience varies by block. Expect basic shared/residential living and verify current room allocation.
student_mode_summary.choice_advice: Choose a branch you are willing to study because branch change is not available for the 2026 batch.
student_mode_summary.label: Official facts combined with cautious student-reported context

decision_risks.risk: Historical cutoffs are not guarantees for 2026. New branches, seat changes, applicant demand and counselling rules can shift ranks.
JoSAA and CSAB rank semantics differ: CSAB PDF ranks are All India CRL even for category rows, while JoSAA reserved-category rows use category ranks.
The 2024 AI quota label requires verification before predictive modelling.
Mathematics and Computing is new for 2026 and lacks historical cutoff evidence.
Branch change is unavailable for the 2026 batch, increasing the importance of branch choice at admission.
Official placement outcomes are overall and cannot be safely converted into branch-specific placement probabilities.
Hostel and mess costs can change and mess expenditure is not a fixed annual fee.
Campus is about 22 km from Kozhikode city; late-night travel may be less convenient.
Scholarships require separate application, verification and sanction; availability and benefits are not guaranteed.

data_gaps: Official completed 2026 JoSAA/CSAB cutoff rounds are not available in this package; 2023-2025 are historical evidence only.
The uploaded 2024 JoSAA subset includes 28 rows with blank opening and closing ranks; these are documented as unavailable combinations and omitted from numeric cutoff exports.
Current authoritative AICTE institute ID, UGC category and NAAC grade/score/validity were not verified and remain null.
Exact NBA validity dates were not obtained for Engineering Physics; Biotechnology renewal after 2023-2026 is unclear.
Branch-specific placement percentages, eligible/registered counts, PPOs, internships and stipend data were not available in the NIRF source.
The official placement notice’s exact recruitment year is not exposed in the retrieved page text.
Scholarship amount, income limit, deadline and documents are incomplete for most cycle-specific schemes.
Mess cost is an advance/consumption-based item, so a reliable four-year hostel-and-mess total was not calculated.
Company-specific role and branch-eligibility records were not published in the official recruiter notice.
Qualitative evidence is limited and self-selected; it cannot establish a campus-wide numerical satisfaction score.
Mathematics and Computing is new in the 2026 seat matrix and has no historical 2023-2025 cutoff rows in the uploaded data.

conflicts.source_2_value: 2026 seat matrix uses KERALA / Other Than KERALA
conflicts.possible_reason: The merged historical dataset may normalize 2024 outside-state rows as All India instead of Other State.
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Compare a sample of 2024 records against the official JoSAA 2024 archive and decide whether AI should be mapped to Other State for NITC.

conflicts.source_2_value: ₹20,000 credit required at beginning of every semester in note
conflicts.possible_reason: Initial advance and minimum recurring credit may be intentionally different.
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Confirm with 2026 admission instructions/hostel office before calculating annual mess cost.

conflicts.source_1_value: Profile narrative says 13 UG programmes plus ITEP with total intake 1328
conflicts.source_2_value: Engineering/architecture seat table totals 1278 capacity + 34 female supernumerary = 1312
conflicts.possible_reason: Narrative total likely includes the separate ITEP programme and/or uses a different counting convention.
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Do not merge ITEP into engineering admission inventory; verify ITEP intake separately if the product later includes it.

conflicts.source_2_value: Institution home/notice may publish other placement percentages or 1000+ offers
conflicts.possible_reason: Different denominators and measures (students, offers, eligible students, graduating cohort).
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Keep NIRF cohort outcomes separate from institute placement-office percentages and offers.

manual_verification_required: Confirm the institution identity, main-campus address and JoSAA institute code 206.
Confirm that no off-campus or ITEP records were accidentally mixed into the engineering dataset.
Recheck all 13 programme names and 2026 programme codes against JoSAA.
Recheck 2026 programme intake, female-supernumerary totals and category splits; grand totals should be 1,278 capacity + 34 supernumerary = 1,312.
Verify whether 2024 quota value AI in the uploaded JoSAA dataset should be normalized to Other State for NIT Calicut.
Confirm that the 28 blank-rank 2024 JoSAA source rows represent unavailable/vacant combinations and should remain excluded from numeric prediction inputs.
Spot-check at least 5 JoSAA rows per year/category against the official archive, despite the dataset being user-verified.
Spot-check every NIT Calicut page range in CSAB PDFs: Round 1 pages 107-127; Round 2 pages 52-59; Round 3 pages 36-40.
Confirm CSAB category ranks are correctly treated as All India CRL as stated in each PDF.
Confirm exact 2026 JoSAA eligibility (Class XII/top-20 percentile wording) from current business rules before publication.
Confirm that branch change remains unavailable for the 2026 batch.
Confirm current NBA status for Biotechnology after the 2023-2026 period and obtain the exact Engineering Physics validity dates.
Confirm AICTE/UGC/NAAC fields; no current authoritative grade/ID was imported.
Recheck B.Tech, B.Arch and dual-degree fee pages and calculated programme totals.
Clarify the hostel ₹25,000 first-semester advance versus ₹20,000 recurring-credit note before computing annual mess cost.
Confirm E-Grantz eligibility and sanction process; do not promise fee/mess waiver before approval.
Open each 2026-27 scholarship notice when published and capture exact income limits, benefits, deadlines, documents and renewal rules.
Confirm the exact placement cycle/year of the “1000+ placements / 188 companies” institute notice.
Extract branch-specific placement reports from the official CCD portal if they are available in a machine-readable or reviewable format.
Confirm company-specific roles, eligible branches, packages and selection counts; none were inferred as official.
Confirm hostel allocation language for the 2026 reporting instructions; the JoSAA profile statement is an institution-published claim, not an admission guarantee.
Recheck each listed club for current activity, official leadership and recruitment process.
Confirm map coordinates and travel times using a current map; official sources provide distances but not all journey times.
Review all student-experience links for availability, date and speaker/student identity before publishing summaries.
Check all source links for breakage and archive important official PDFs.
Confirm that 2026 official cutoffs are still unavailable/incomplete before using historical trends for prediction.
